book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
Historical Excursus on the After History of the Council.
title: Historical Excursus on the After History of the Council.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: Pope Vigilius died on his way home, but not until, as we have seen, he had accepted and approved the action of the council in doing exactly that which he “by the authority of the Apostolic See” in his Constitutum had forbidden it to do.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: He died at the end of 554 or the beginning of 555.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: Pelagius I., who succeeded him in the See of Rome, likewise confirmed the Acts of the Fifth Synod.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: The council however was not received in all parts of the West, although it had obtained the approval of the Pope.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: It was bitterly opposed in the whole of the north of Italy, in England, France, and Spain, and also in Africa and Asia.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: The African opposition died out by 559, but Milan was in schism until 571, when Pope Justin II. published his “Henoticon.”

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: In Istria the matter was still more serious, and when in 607 the bishop of Aquileia-Grado with those of his suffragans who were subject to the Empire made their submission and were reconciled to the Church, the other bishops of his jurisdiction set up a schismatical Patriarchate at old Aquileia, and this schism continued till the Council of Aquileia in 700.

verses.book: The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
The Fifth Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Constantinople.
verses.text: Council of Constantinople was received all the world over as the Fifth Ecumenical Council; and was fully recognized as such by the Sixth Council in 680.
